The baseline layout makes financial planning critical from the outset. The Unified National Refund Window FAST applies a strict refund timeline dictated uniformly across institutions like FAST, NUST, and GIKI under the higher education regulatory guidelines. This structured timeline regulates all institutional withdrawals: 1 Up to Day 10 of Classes: Full tuition recovery window (the primary admission processing fee remains completely non-refundable). 2 From Day 11 up to Day 15 of Classes: Tuition recovery drops to eighty percent of total base value. 3 From Day 16 up to Day 20 of Classes: Tuition recovery drops to sixty percent of total base value. 4 From Day 21 up to Day 30 of Classes: The final legal threshold, allowing a fifty percent tuition return. 5 From Day 31 of Classes Onward: Absolute forfeiture window. No tuition refunds are processed under any circumstance. The Strategic Multi-Offer Playbook: If you are holding an active FAST acceptance offer while anxiously awaiting a late-cycle merit call from NUST or LUMS, you can securely deposit your initial dues at FAST to protect your seat. This allows you to safely transition and recover the vast majority of your tuition base if your primary choice comes through within the first two weeks—provided you execute your withdrawal cleanly before the 30-day cliff. Common Mistakes Students Make Most fee-related stress happens because students calculate only the visible semester fee and ignore the full degree cost. • Ignoring localized hostel, dining, and seasonal transport costs. • Assuming financial assistance will arrive before the first installment deadline. • Forgetting that admission processing fee is completely non-refundable. • Failing to account for per-credit-hour costs tied to summer terms or grade remediation. • Missing critical refund deadlines while waiting on secondary university selectors. • Relying on outdated social media fee screenshots instead of tracking the live official web portal. Quick Summary The true cost of a FAST education encompasses admission fees, refundable safety deposits, core tuition scaling between Rs. 200,000 and Rs. 255,000 per semester, and prospective per-credit-hour remediation fees. Ensure you insulate your enrollment journey by tracking the HEC multi-tier refund timeline carefully (which closes completely after Day 30) and building a realistic four-year financial projection model before committing your resources. Next Financial Assistance & Aid Strategies →

title: Financial Aid
content: Home / Finances / Financial Assistance Finances FAST Financial Assistance — How FA Works & What It Really Costs FAST Financial Assistance (FA) is a critical survival mechanism for many students, but it is essential to look past the immediate relief to the long-term reality. FA is structured as Qarz-e-Hasana—an interest-free loan—rather than a free grant. Understanding the lifecycle of this debt is mandatory before you commit. Defining the Assistance Framework Financial Assistance at FAST is strictly need-based. The assistance percentage scales dynamically based on documented family income, household assets, and dependency burdens, rather than academic rank or entry-test percentiles. Repayment Structure FA functions as Qarz-e-Hasana. It is an interest-free loan, not a scholarship grant. It is capital that must be returned in full. Eligibility Gates Maintaining assistance requires consistent academic health: you must avoid failing courses and hold a CGPA consistently above the minimum floor (typically 2.50). The Exit Clause Repayment obligations activate either 3 months after graduation or upon your first employment, whichever event occurs first. To apply, students must complete the official FA application available at nu.edu.pk/Admissions/Scholarship. Ensure you also secure the FA application PDF from the university's official download repository. The Application & Verification Pipeline The application process is thorough and evidence-heavy. For a successful submission, treat this like a formal financial audit: Required Document Format Purpose Income Proof Official Payslips / Bank Statements Verifies exact monthly household income. Utility Bills Latest Electricity / Gas Bills Serves as a secondary proof of residence and living standard. Housing Proof Rental Agreement / Ownership Docs Validates dependency burdens and accommodation costs. Supplemental Indicators Disability Certificates / Report Cards Bolsters the case but is not a core requirement. 1 Initial Application: Apply during the admission cycle by completing the FA form in-flow. If you missed this window, download the form and submit it physically at your designated campus office. 2 Mandatory Panel Interview: You and your father/guardian must attend an on-campus interview. The panel probes every facet of your household’s financial management. Honesty and precise specificity during this conversation are the primary factors determining your final assistance percentage. 3 The Mid-Semester Bridge: FA awards are typically finalized mid-semester. Consequently, you must arrange the initial installment personally. Consult the accounts office early regarding a payment plan to potentially reduce the first bill (e.g., shifting a Rs. 255,000 total to an initial Rs. 150,000 payment). The Repayment Arithmetic Before accepting any assistance, perform the math. FA makes FAST accessible (by deferring costs), not cheap (by removing them). If your long-term plan involves exiting Pakistan immediately post-graduation, be aware that this repayment schedule effectively functions as a monthly loan repayment leash. Assistance Tier Estimated Total Debt Post-Graduation Monthly Burden 60% Assistance ~Rs. 960,000 – 1,000,000 ~Rs. 21,000/month (over 4 years) 25% Assistance ~Rs. 280,000 ~Rs. 10,000 – 15,000/month Financial Takeaway: Model these figures against your projected early-career salary. The goal is to ensure the obligation doesn't eat up your financial freedom during your most critical early-career growth phase. Common FA Mistakes • Assuming FA is a Grant: Failing to account for the repayment schedule as a future liability. • Neglecting Maintenance CGPA: Losing financial aid in later semesters due to academic dips below the 2.50 floor. • Assuming Instant Disbursement: Being unprepared for the mid-semester gap where you must pay the first installment before aid arrives. • Vague Documentation: Submitting incomplete evidence during the panel interview, which leads to lower assistance percentages. Quick Summary FAST Financial Assistance is a need-based Qarz-e-Hasana loan system. It is designed to bridge the gap between your current family capacity and the university’s tuition requirements, but it requires diligent evidence collection, a mandatory guardian interview, and a committed repayment schedule post-graduation. Always model the 4-year monthly repayment plan before committing to ensure the obligation is sustainable for your post-degree career path. Next Scholarships & Merit-Based Aid →

title: Scholarships
content: Home / Finances / Scholarships Finances FAST University Scholarship Merit 2026: Honhaar, PEEF & OSAF FAST students can explore multiple financial support channels, including government scholarships, university-linked aid, external trusts, and merit-based tuition relief. The important thing is not to depend on only one option. Apply broadly, verify every deadline, and confirm whether different scholarships can be combined. Scholarships vs Financial Assistance Before comparing funding channels, you must understand the distinction between general scholarships and institutional Financial Assistance. While FAST Financial Assistance functions strictly as a need-based interest-free loan (Qarz-e-Hasana) that requires mandatory repayment post-graduation, scholarship programs operate under independent, non-repayable, or trust-funded frameworks. Financial Assistance Need-based university loan structured as Qarz-e-Hasana, requiring full post-graduation repayment. Scholarships Government-backed or endowment-funded pathways that remain non-repayable if compliance is met. Merit Relief Full or partial tuition waivers granted explicitly to top-ranked entrants based on entry-test performance. The Punjab Honhaar Scholarship The Punjab Honhaar Scholarship is designed as a flagship full-ride government scheme covering 100% of tuition costs through a shared layout between the state and the institution across a four-year bachelor's path. The Lived Reality at FAST: The actual deployment of Honhaar funds on campuses has historically proven rocky and inconsistent. At various intervals, university departments claimed the allocation covered only 50% of fees or noted that state funds had not yet cleared. Conflicting administrative statements regarding who made the official HEC eligibility lists left many candidates without formal award letters, even as companion campuses like Islamabad and Chiniot held formal disbursement ceremonies and distributed cheques. The 93%+ Private Merit Floor: Furthermore, the merit requirement at private universities under Honhaar is brutal. Because private institutional quotas are highly competitive, multiple applicants have faced rejection due to private-sector merit thresholds scaling above 93%—a floor significantly higher than the standard public university baseline. Do not factor this into your financial math unless your aggregate is exceptionally high. Endowment and University Aid Instruments If the headline government programs stall, you should immediately diversify your tracking into specific institutional and provincial endowment funds: Funding Channel Target Baseline & Focus Application Workflow & Constraints PEEF (Punjab Educational Endowment Fund) Punjab-domiciled candidates carrying a certified monthly family income under approximately Rs. 60,000. Apply at the start of the academic year directly through the university's HR/accounts office. OSAF Need-based internal relief platform managed via FAST central structures. Applications are invited via an official email circular sent after the semester starts. Detailed rules live at nu.edu.pk/Admissions/Scholarship. FANA & Ihsan Trust Supplementary need-based channels (Ihsan Trust operates as a prominent external charitable trust). Verification protocols, document packets, and coverage limits must be secured via the campus accounts or FA office. The Exclusivity Constraint: Be aware that drawing an active award from an external or government program can automatically disqualify you from internal aid structures like OSAF. Check exclusivity rules with your campus accounts office before filing papers. Top-of-Test Merit Relief For elite performers, FAST offers direct Merit-Based Tuition Relief. This tier awards up to 100% free tuition waivers exclusively to top-ranked entrants who ace the university entry examination. Because this relief functions as a pure merit waiver based on performance rather than income documentation, it features zero post-graduation repayment or loan liabilities. Given the current Rs. 12,000 per credit hour tuition rate, a 100% merit waiver represents a massive financial win, saving top students over Rs. 2,000,000 across their four-year degree compared to relying on loans. The Simultaneous Application Strategy The most critical rule of financial planning at FAST is to apply to every available channel simultaneously: FA, Honhaar, PEEF, OSAF, and Ihsan Trust. These resource pools are not always mutually exclusive. The worst-case outcome of broad filing is a delayed partial award or an administrative adjustment, whereas relying on a single channel can leave you completely exposed if that specific system stalls. Always verify with your campus accounts office to see which funding blocks can legally stack. 1 Secure the Core: Submit your institutional FAST FA paperwork directly during the active admission window. 2 Government Alignment: Complete your state profiles on the official Honhaar portal and double-check tracking lists. 3 Endowment Filing: Process your PEEF documentation through the campus accounts office at the start of the academic cycle. 4 Circular Monitoring: Monitor your student email daily post-enrollment for the official OSAF application link. 5 External Outreach: Coordinate with campus FA officers to submit auxiliary document bundles to external entities like Ihsan Trust. Common Mistakes Students Make • Single-Channel Dependency: Risking your enrollment by applying to only one scholarship track and waiting passively for results. • Missing the Private Merit Reality: Expecting standard public thresholds to apply to private-university Honhaar quotas, where requirements can exceed 93%. • Assuming Instant Cash: Failing to plan a personal cash backup for your first semester fees while waiting for government scholarship checks to clear. • Exclusivity Violations: Mixing multiple awards without verifying compliance rules, which can trigger automatic aid cancellations. Quick Summary FAST's scholarship environment is composed of diverse avenues including the Punjab Honhaar program, PEEF endowment grants, university-tracked OSAF aid, external options like Ihsan Trust, and top-of-test merit waivers. Because administrative delays and high merit cut-offs are common, you should build a secure funding portfolio by applying to all available channels simultaneously, preparing your verified documentation early, and cross-checking your stacking eligibility directly with the campus accounts office. Next The Affordability Calculator & Strategy →

title: FAQs
content: Home / Help & FAQs / FAQ Help & FAQs FAST Admissions & Academics — Frequently Asked Questions Got questions about tests, degrees, attendance, or fees? Here are the straightforward answers to the most common questions applicants and students ask about navigating FAST. Should I take the NU Test or the NTS NAT for FAST? Take the NU Test. FAST allocates the vast majority of its open seats to applicants sitting the in-house NU entry test, making its final closing merit the most forgiving and realistic option to clear. The NTS NAT stream competes for a much smaller, highly restricted pool of reserved seats. Historically, getting in via NAT has required an almost perfect score, and the merit cutoffs fluctuate violently from year to year. Read more detailed breakdowns in Entry Test Streams. Does FAST still accept SAT Subject Tests (SAT-II)? No. The College Board permanently discontinued SAT Subject Tests (SAT-II) globally in 2021. FAST now evaluates applicants using the regular SAT-I reasoning exam on a completely separate merit list. While a few legacy campus sub-sites still accidentally show stale references to SAT-II, you should always ignore them and verify current parameters on the central nu.edu.pk admissions page. See SAT Test Stream for the full SAT admission breakdown. What is the minimum attendance required to sit finals at FAST? You must maintain a minimum of 80% attendance for undergraduate programs and 75% attendance for graduate or postgraduate courses. If you drop below this threshold, the system automatically renders you ineligible to sit the final exam, resulting in an immediate F/A (Fail due to Absence) grade on your transcript. The university only condones up to 20% of absences for genuine, heavily documented emergencies like medical illness. Full rules on the attendance wall, missed-exam boards, and the cheating policy are in Attendance, Missed Exams & Academic Integrity. What CGPA do I need to avoid an academic warning? To stay in good academic standing, you must maintain a minimum cumulative GPA of 2.00 for Bachelor's (BS/BBA) programs, 2.50 for Master's (MS/MBA) degrees, and 3.00 for a Ph.D. If your CGPA drops below these lines at the end of any semester, your official Warning Count goes up by one. If you fail to pull your grades up and hit three consecutive warnings, your university admission will be permanently closed. Read about emergency recovery tactics in Academic Warnings & Probation. What GPA do I need to go abroad for a funded master's? Most reputable international universities enforce a hard 3.0 GPA cutoff just to evaluate your application file. To be genuinely competitive, a 3.3+ GPA loaded with top scores in core computing and math subjects is widely reported as good enough for admissions, while a 3.5+ GPA materially secures your odds for fully funded scholarships. Keep in mind that hitting these target metrics on FAST's brutal grading curve requires far more exhaustion and grind than it would at NUST, LUMS, ITU, or a standard public university. The SGPA/CGPA formulas and the 3.0 foreign-master's cutoff are detailed in Grading System & GPA Dynamics. Is FAST's Financial Assistance a grant or a loan? It is structured strictly as Qarz-e-Hasana—an interest-free loan, not a free grant. You are legally required to start paying back the university three months after your official graduation date or as soon as you secure your first job, whichever comes first. The entire balance must be cleared within 4 years. For instance, if you receive a standard 60% Financial Assistance package, you will graduate owing roughly Rs. 1,000,000, which translates into a fixed bill of roughly Rs. 21,000 per month for forty-eight months right after you step off campus. See Financial Aid & Qarz-e-Hasana for the full repayment breakdown. Can I afford FAST if my family income is around Rs. 80,000/month? It is exceptionally difficult and entirely dependent on stacking multiple aid pipelines perfectly. If you try it, you must apply to the FAST loan program, the Honhaar Scholarship, PEEF, OSAF, and the Ihsan Trust simultaneously while mapping out your post-grad repayment model. You should absolutely keep a solid backup option open—such as UET, PUCIT, ITU, or NED—where you can easily absorb the semester fees without heavy debt. If attending FAST requires your family to take out commercial bank loans on top of internal university aid, choosing a cheaper public college and building killer coding skills on your own is a far smarter path for a local tech career. Is the gender rules viral post about FAST real? No—it is pure online satire. The viral memes listing extreme fine systems for sitting with classmates or talking across genders are jokes. FAST operates like any normal, co-educational university in Pakistan, adhering to standard cultural boundaries and customary social courtesies. While the student stress and academic anxiety the meme mocks are very real, the crazy rules themselves do not exist. Is BS Computer Science better than BS Cybersecurity if I'm unsure? Yes, absolutely. A standard BS CS degree covers the entire landscape of engineering, AI, data architecture, and software development, allowing you to easily pivot into cybersecurity later via electives or industry certifications. Trying to pivot the other way around is significantly harder because a cybersecurity degree locks you into a narrow curriculum. Furthermore, the junior entry-level security market inside Pakistan is quite thin. Only specialize early if you possess a deeply researched, verified passion for the domain. How long do I have to complete my BS at FAST? Following Higher Education Commission (HEC) regulations, you have a maximum of six years to finish your 4-year Bachelor's degree. While you can technically request a one-year emergency extension, the university's Statutory Bodies only grant this for severe, completely unavoidable life events—such as documented medical conditions, a death in your immediate family, maternity, or the observation of Iddat. title: About Us
content: Home / About Us About Us Our Mission Getting into FAST is a dream for many, but the application process from entry test criteria to merit calculations is often confusing and lacks clear, centralized guidance. Thousands of applicants enter the process every year with questions that aren't answered by the official portal. Our mission is to bridge that gap. We created FAST Wiki to be the resource we wish we had: a transparent, student-led, and comprehensive guide for everyone thinking about, or currently applying to, FAST. We’ve synthesized the confusion into clear paths so your only concern is performing your best on test day, not figuring out the application system. The Founders Muhammad Talha Hamid Getting into FAST in 2024 was a journey but not an easy one. Like many, I started with zero roadmap and even less guidance on how to navigate the complex application process. About a year later, I started a WhatsApp community to address this issue and that quickly grew to over 1,000+ members. Later on, realizing that manual support wasn't scalable, I partnered with Ahmed to build this platform. I’ve poured every lesson learned from thousands of applicant queries into this site, creating the resource I wish I had on day one. I hope this wiki makes your journey to FAST a little bit clearer. Ahmed Akhtar As a FAST student, I know how frustrating it is when essential information is scattered across WhatsApp groups, seniors, and official pages. Finding a simple answer shouldn't take hours. When Talha and I started FAST-Wiki, my focus was to transform that chaos into a clean, intuitive platform. From the interface design to the content structure, we built this to give both applicants and current students one clear, stress-free place to find exactly what they need.
